caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied 4-digit 5418 occupation mix is agency-weighted and not a direct-mail plant census.

inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Physical production, materials and postage work are likely concentrated in the residual; its fraction is an informed proxy, not observed NAICS 541860 occupational microdata.
inputs.pi_dist: No published NAICS 541860 pass-through study was located; this is a pricing-model judgment.
inputs.pi_moat: Applies to a differentiated target; a commodity mail house can be materially lower.
inputs.t50_years: The survey spans print segments, not direct-mail-only firms.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: This measures effective operating adoption, not any employee trying a general-purpose AI tool.
inputs.historical_analogs: No direct historical AI adoption series for 541860 was found.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Neither source is a representative NAICS 541860 owner-age census.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: This is a directional diligence flag, not proof of a NAICS-specific shortage.
inputs.active_consolidators: This is not a comprehensive count of all buyers or a count of NAICS-coded acquirers.
inputs.buy_mult: S4 reports owner earnings/SDE, not EBITDA, and covers paper manufacturing/printing rather than exact NAICS 541860.
inputs.buy_mult: S5 provides actual direct-mail EBITDA scale and buyer interest but no disclosed transaction price or multiple.
inputs.exit_mult: This is a target-quality scenario, not a market-wide multiple; the cited transaction data are SDE-based and proxy-only.
inputs.pricing_structure: Actual contract terms and postage pass-through must be confirmed in target diligence.
S2 verifies the 2016-2025 Marketing Mail volume decline but is a USPS Postal Facts table, not the USPS OIG source claimed for the lower-cost-online-alternatives attribution.
The approximately $6.0 million sales and $0.9 million EBITDA direct-mail listing is not in cited S5 (NDP January 2026); it appears in NDP's April 2026 newsletter.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $264,563 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541860); class <5: 924 firms, avg revenue/firm $552,198 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $86,419 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 254 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,034,370 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $318,379 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 185 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,671,557 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $574,599 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 199 firms, avg revenue/firm $12,029,618 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,882,635 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 62 firms, avg revenue/firm $31,234,274 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $4,888,164 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 28 firms, avg revenue/firm $122,338,179 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $19,145,925 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 261

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: AI can replace much of creative, data, quoting, prepress and administration, but actual direct-mail operators have a large unlisted physical production, inserting, finishing and postal-operations wage bill. The 5418 role proxy is reinterpreted conservatively through the residual rather than treated as a pure agency workforce.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541860 ($2,490,801,764) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541860 ($9,462,087,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics pass. The low-confidence selective/kill thesis remains useful and rubric-consistent, with capture and terminal-demand mechanisms kept separate and no material double count. Publication must retain all authored proxy and uncertainty caveats plus the two citation-attribution caveats identified here.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual
sources.what_it_supports: Exact 2022 scope for 541860, and its boundary with physical distribution in 541870, printing and mailing-list publishing.

sources.title: USPS Postal Facts: A Decade of Facts and Figures
sources.what_it_supports: Official annual Marketing Mail volume series, including 80.9bn pieces in FY2016 and 56.8bn in FY2025.

sources.title: AI Adoption in the Printing Industry: From Curiosity to Competitive Advantage
sources.what_it_supports: 2025 print-provider survey evidence on AI use in estimating, prepress, customer communications and workflow automation, plus fragmented adoption and staffing effects.

sources.title: Paper Manufacturing & Printing Business Valuation Benchmarks
sources.what_it_supports: Sold small printing-business owner-earnings/SDE valuation data: 2.45x median and 2.57x average for 2021-25, with the metric explicitly identified as owner earnings rather than EBITDA.

sources.title: New Direction Partners January 2026 Newsletter
sources.what_it_supports: Printing/direct-mail M&A practitioner commentary: retirement-age traditional owners, differentiated data-driven direct mail, buyer interest and direct-mail transaction activity.

sources.title: Most Small-Business Owners Lack a Succession Plan
sources.what_it_supports: 2024 U.S. small-business-owner survey on sale, transfer, closure and succession planning; used only as a broad owner-demographic proxy.

title: Direct Mail Advertising
